prompt:
Assign GRADE certainty ratings to each evidence item below.
Ratings: HIGH (systematic review of RCTs, low heterogeneity), MODERATE (RCT with some concerns, or consistent observational), LOW (observational studies, high heterogeneity, indirect evidence), VERY_LOW (case reports, expert opinion, serious limitations).

For each item, output ONLY the number and rating, one per line:
1. HIGH
2. MODERATE
...

EVIDENCE:
1. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: PEVA18 showed inadequate transmittance and high stiffness after 2000 h of DH aging, discouraging its use as encapsulant.
2. [SILVER] Source: Enhancement of resistance against damp heat aging through co | Statement: Results encourage the PV industry to reduce VA content in PEVA to 24% for better encapsulation response against DH aging.
3. [GOLD] Source: Investigating the Crosslinking, Degradation, and Adhesion Be | Statement: Encapsulant crosslinking increased and crystallinity decreased under hot-aerobic aging at 90 degrees C and 22% RH without UV or crosslinking initiator
4. [GOLD] Source: Investigating the Crosslinking, Degradation, and Adhesion Be | Statement: Adhesion energy Gc decreased after 5000 h of both hot-dry (90 degrees C, ~1% RH) and hot-humid (90 degrees C, 60% RH) aging on coupon-level cell/encap
5. [GOLD] Source: Investigating the Crosslinking, Degradation, and Adhesion Be | Statement: POE coupons demonstrated the best adhesion stability under thermal aging, followed by EPE composites and then EVA.
